title: Cragmaw Hideout

location.name: Cragmaw Hideout
location.description: A cave complex hidden in the hills northwest of the Triboar Trail. A stream flows out of the cave mouth. The goblins of the Cragmaw tribe use this as a base for their raids.

read_aloud: Following the goblin trail, you find a large cave in a hillside five miles from the scene of the ambush. A shallow stream flows out of the cave mouth, which is screened by dense briar thickets. A narrow, dry path leads into the cave alongside the stream.

encounters.trigger_condition: Party enters the cave without scouting

enemies.position: Goblin blind — thicket 30 feet from cave entrance, watching the trail

encounters.tactics: These lookouts fire arrows and then flee into the cave to warn the others.
encounters.description: Two goblin sentries watch the cave entrance from a hidden blind in the thickets.

enemies.position: Chained to iron rods in a side cave. They bark and snap at intruders.

encounters.tactics: Wolves are chained and can only reach creatures within 10 feet. If freed or if chains break (AC 20, HP 10), wolves attack the nearest target.
encounters.description: Three wolves chained in the kennel cave. They alert goblins upstream.

enemies.position: In a large cave with cooking fires and sleeping pallets
enemies.special: Yeemik — second-in-command goblin, holds Sildar Hallwinter hostage

encounters.tactics: Yeemik threatens to kill Sildar (pushing him off a ledge) unless the party agrees to kill Klarg, the bugbear leader. If the party attacks, Yeemik shoves Sildar off the ledge (1d6 fall damage) on his first turn.
encounters.description: Yeemik's den. He has Sildar Hallwinter as a hostage and wants to overthrow Klarg.

enemies.special: Klarg — bugbear chieftain of the Cragmaw goblins at this hideout

enemies.position: Klarg's pet wolf, Ripper, beside him

encounters.tactics: Klarg is arrogant and attacks immediately. He fights to the death. If the goblins upstream released a flood (dam trap), the stream passage is dangerous.
encounters.description: Klarg's lair — the final encounter in the hideout. Defeating Klarg secures the cave.

npcs.name: Sildar Hallwinter
dialog_hooks: Gundren and his map were taken to Cragmaw Castle by order of King Grol
He is a member of the Lords' Alliance, sent to bring order to Phandalin
He is looking for Iarno Albrek, a fellow Alliance agent who disappeared near Phandalin two months ago
He will join the party to Phandalin and offer 50 gp for safe escort

quest.name: Rescue Gundren Rockseeker
quest.objective: Find Cragmaw Castle and rescue Gundren — he has a map to Wave Echo Cave
quest.reward: Sildar will pay the party well and assist however he can
quest.destination: Cragmaw Castle (location unknown — must be discovered)

loot.location: Stolen goods in Klarg's cave — stamped with a blue lion. Return to Linene in Phandalin for 50 gp reward.

skill_checks.success: Notice the goblin blind before approaching the cave entrance.
skill_checks.failure: Walk past without noticing. Goblins get first attack.

skill_checks.success: Sneak past the wolves without alerting them.
skill_checks.failure: Wolves bark, alerting goblins deeper in the cave.

traps.name: Flood Trap
traps.trigger_description: Goblins on the bridge can release a dam, flooding the stream passage
traps.damage: Swept 30 feet downstream, knocked prone. DC 10 STR save or take 1d6 bludgeoning.
traps.effect: The flood fills the passage waist-deep. Can be used twice (two dams).
traps.detection: The stream level rises before the flood hits — Perception DC 12 gives 1 round warning.
traps.disarm: Destroying the dam releases the flood harmlessly before goblins can use it.

dm_notes: This is a multi-room dungeon. Run it room by room — don't dump all encounters at once. The Yeemik hostage situation is the first real social/moral choice. If they negotiate with Yeemik, they can avoid fighting his goblins and get Sildar back safely. Sildar is the main exposition NPC — he connects Chapter 1 to Chapters 2 and 3. Make sure he delivers his dialog hooks.
